join_order.build_structure: flat_bool_array[1500001] indexed by c_custkey (dense domain, O(1) lookup)
join_order.output: qualifying_orders hash map, ~630K entries (c_mktsegment+date combined selectivity ~0.042)
aggregation groups keyed by (l_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_shippriority), ~150K groups

filter_pushdown.note: combined with customer semi-join reduces to ~630K
zone map skips ~46% of blocks (those fully before 1995-03-15)

data_structures.customer_filter: flat_bool_array(capacity=1500001) — dense int32 c_custkey domain [1..1500000]; set is_building[c_custkey]=true for each BUILDING customer; O(1) probe via direct index
data_structures.orders_join: compact_hash_map(open_addressing, pre_sized=1048576, load_factor=0.60) key=int32_t o_orderkey value={int32_t o_orderdate, int32_t o_shippriority}; fibonacci hash: key*0x9E3779B97F4A7C15ULL
data_structures.aggregation: thread_local compact_hash_map per thread (open_addressing, pre_sized=262144) key=(int32_t l_orderkey, int32_t o_orderdate, int32_t o_shippriority) value=double revenue_sum; final sequential merge into global map after parallel scan
data_structures.topk: min_heap(size=10) keyed by (revenue DESC, o_orderdate ASC); extract after aggregation merge

execution_phases.name: customer_scan_and_bitset_build
execution_phases.description: Single-threaded sequential scan of customer/c_mktsegment.bin (1.5M × 1 byte = 1.5MB). For each row where raw==1 (BUILDING), read c_custkey.bin and set is_building[c_custkey]=true. Sequential, small data — parallelism overhead not worth it.

execution_phases.name: orders_scan_join_customer_build_orders_hashmap
execution_phases.description: Load o_orderdate_zonemap. Binary search for last qualifying block (o_orderdate < 9204, ascending-sorted). Skip the trailing ~51% of blocks entirely. For qualifying blocks: scan o_orderdate.bin + o_custkey.bin + o_orderkey.bin + o_shippriority.bin fused. Filter: o_orderdate < 9204 AND is_building[o_custkey]. Insert qualifying rows into compact orders hash map. Parallel morsel scan, lock-free inserts via pre-partition or single-threaded build (630K rows, ~15ms single-thread).
execution_phases.zonemap_skip: skip block i if entry[i].min_val >= 9204.0 (ascending sort → binary search for cutoff block, skip all blocks beyond it, ~51% skipped)
execution_phases.parallel_note: Parallel morsel scan on qualifying blocks; hash map build via partitioned insert (partition by o_orderkey hash) or single-threaded given small output size (630K)

execution_phases.name: lineitem_scan_join_orders_aggregate
execution_phases.description: Load l_shipdate_zonemap. Skip blocks where max_val <= 9204.0 (~46% blocks, leftmost part of sorted file). For remaining blocks: fused scan of l_shipdate.bin + l_orderkey.bin + l_extendedprice.bin + l_discount.bin. Filter: l_shipdate > 9204. Probe orders_hash_map[l_orderkey]. On hit: compute revenue = l_extendedprice * (1 - l_discount). Accumulate into thread-local aggregation hash map keyed by (l_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_shippriority). This is the dominant phase.
execution_phases.zonemap_skip: skip block i if entry[i].max_val <= 9204.0 (ascending sort → skip all leading blocks fully before cutoff, ~46% skipped)
execution_phases.morsel_rationale: L3=44MB / 64 threads ≈ 687KB per thread; 4 cols × 32K rows × ~6 bytes avg = ~768KB, fits in L3 slice

execution_phases.name: merge_aggregates_and_topk
execution_phases.description: Sequential merge of 64 thread-local hash maps into global result. Then apply top-10 min-heap selection: iterate global map, maintain heap of size 10 ordered by (revenue DESC, o_orderdate ASC). Output 10 rows.

parallelism.phase_2_parallel: morsel-driven parallel scan of qualifying order blocks; single-threaded hash map build (630K entries fast to build sequentially, avoids synchronization overhead)
parallelism.phase_3_parallel: morsel-driven parallel scan of qualifying lineitem blocks; each thread owns a private aggregation hash map; merge sequentially after all threads complete; no synchronization during scan

index_usage.layout: [uint32_t num_blocks=150][{double min_val, double max_val, uint32_t row_count, uint32_t _pad} × 150]
index_usage.strategy: binary search on ascending-sorted min_vals to find last qualifying block index; skip all subsequent blocks

index_usage.layout: [uint32_t num_blocks=600][{double min_val, double max_val, uint32_t row_count, uint32_t _pad} × 600]
index_usage.strategy: binary search on ascending-sorted max_vals to find first qualifying block index; skip all preceding blocks

indexes_not_used.reason: Full customer scan needed anyway to find BUILDING customers; flat bool array is O(1) and avoids hash collision overhead for the dense integer domain [1..1500000]
Runtime hash map of qualifying orders is built in phase 2 (~630K entries); using the pre-built 15M-entry disk index would be slower to load and probe vs. a warm in-memory 630K hash map

memory_estimates.lineitem_thread_local_agg_maps: 64 threads × 262144 slots × ~20 bytes = ~336 MB total (fits in 376 GB RAM)

estimated_cost.bottleneck: lineitem scan over ~32.4M rows (4 columns, ~778 MB after zone-map skip) across 64 threads; random access into 630K-entry orders hash map (mostly L3-resident)
